title: Adding timeout parameter to container.wait() and fixing interval type
body:
When calling wait() on a container it was impossible to set a timeout for waiting on the container. This adds the timeout parameter and sets it accordingly when doing the request.

Also the interval parameter was described to be an integer, but the API documentation states it to be a string, e.g. '5s'.

I am not sure what to make of: 
> Returns:
> "Error" key has a dictionary value with the key "Message".

The call should either raise or return an Integer which is the exit code of the process running in the container.
I personally find that confusing.

I also tried to clarify what the returned value actually is below. 

Feedback highly appreciated.

comments.body:
@mutax linter is complaining over a space but LGTM

```diff
--- podman/tests/unit/test_container.py
+++ podman/tests/unit/test_container.py
@@ -227,7 +227,7 @@
         container = Container(attrs=FIRST_CONTAINER, client=self.client.api)
         container.wait(condition="exited", interval="23s")
         self.assertTrue(adapter.called_once)
-        self.maxDiff=None
+        self.maxDiff = None
```
whoops :/ will update later and fix indents - sorry, only executed unit tests at the end
